title: Currency conversion provider catalogue
description: The 3 exchange-rate providers supported by ExchangeRateResolver. Tenants using multi-currency (Medium+ tier) choose a provider at settings time. openexchangerates is the default; fixer_io is an alternative; ecb_daily is always-available fallback + regulator-preferred for EU jurisdictions.

providers.regulator_preference: acceptable for USD-anchored tenants + reference-rate reporting
providers.cost_note: Paid API — configured via TRANSACTION_OPENEXCHANGERATES_APP_ID Doppler secret
notes: Historical rate lookup available via /historical/{date}.json endpoint
Free tier limited to 1000 requests/month; production tenants use paid tier

providers.regulator_preference: acceptable for EU-anchored tenants; ECB-source rates on the highest tier
providers.cost_note: Paid API — configured via TRANSACTION_FIXER_IO_API_KEY Doppler secret
notes: Higher tiers offer real-time rates + more historical data + ECB-official rate sourcing
Historical rate lookup available via /{YYYY-MM-DD} endpoint

providers.regulator_preference: PREFERRED for EU jurisdictions — official ECB reference rates are the regulator's own source. Regulator audits use ECB rates by default.
providers.cost_note: Free — no auth, no cost, no rate limit
notes: The 'fallback' provider — always available regardless of tenant settings
Regulator-preferred for EU tenants (VAT reporting, IFRS reporting)
Only daily updates — less useful for high-frequency FX tenants
EUR-based; rate lookups for non-EUR pairs cross-triangulate via EUR (USD-GBP = USD-EUR / GBP-EUR)

fallback_chain.description: First attempt: use whichever provider the tenant selected in settings (openexchangerates default).
Fallback attempt: if the primary is unreachable OR returns an error, try ECB Daily. Always available.
Last resort: use the last cached rate for the currency pair, even if stale beyond max_stale_seconds. Refuses when the cache is empty (returns TRANSACTION_EXCHANGE_RATE_UNAVAILABLE).

notes: The ExchangeRateResolver walks the fallback chain in order — every failure is logged with the provider slug + error code + duration for ops visibility.
Rate captures are IMMUTABLE after transaction commit — the historical report will always use the frozen rate from the transaction row, regardless of what the provider's current rate is.
Regulator preference: EU tenants should configure ecb_daily as their primary for IFRS + VAT audit ease. US tenants can use openexchangerates (USD-anchored is the natural fit).
Rate caching TTL is 1h (transaction.exchange_rate.cache_ttl_seconds). Max stale is 6h (transaction.exchange_rate.max_stale_seconds). Beyond max stale, the resolver forces a fresh fetch or fails hard.

Failure modes across all three: (a) network timeout → retry with backoff via the ExchangeRateResolver's built-in retry; (b) auth failure (401) → circuit-breaker opens fast; (c) rate limit (429) → wait per Retry-After header then retry; (d) invalid currency pair → fail hard (TRANSACTION_EXCHANGE_RATE_UNAVAILABLE for the specific pair).

Regulators + auditors may request the provider slug + rate source for a specific historical transaction — the rate_provider field on the transaction row records this. Adjust the FreezeExchangeRateOnCrossCurrencyTransaction hook to record which provider actually served the rate (may differ from the tenant's primary if the fallback triggered).
